hard.next_step: June 30, 2026: shared Business and Technical questionnaire. We would appreciate if you could send us your responses by July 10th. Dear Hemendra, Please find attached a Business and Technical questionnaire. We would appreciate if you could send us your responses by July 10 th . It will help us understand whether the requested business, functional, non-functional, and technical specs are supported by your software solution and how. Specifically: PPA_Business_Questionaire.xlsx - Business Questionnaire On sheet “ Business Questionnaire Reqs ” you will find: Functional / Business requirements related to both Digitalization and AI Functionalities across the entire Procurement lifecycle. Kindly indicate to each whether it is covered by your software. Non Functional requirements, covering more generic areas of the procurement lifecycle. Kindly indicate to each whether it is covered by your software. On sheet “ User Matrix ” you may find an analysis of all roles involved in a procurement process. Kindly fulfill the empty columns namely: Type of license required, and License Cost (budget). On sheets “ Reporting 1 ”, “ Reporting 2 ” and “ Procurement Plan ” you will find some requested reports. Kindly indicate, if relevant information report can be exported by your software. Technical Questionnaire.docx - Technical Questionnaire We kindly request you responses to each technical question so we could gain a better understanding of the AI architecture and technical capabilities of your software. Following our last conversation, we are also pending a demo of Zycus application. Thank you in advance. Please let us know in case any clarification is required. The next step is to schedule a tailored demo focused on their tendering process, from supplier registration to sourcing event creation, supplier participation, evaluation, negotiation, and award. Alongside this, share a pricing questionnaire to help them estimate the potential cost. Internally, we should also check implementation support options for Greece, including local integrator or Big Four partner possibilities. They have also gotten back to us with a techincal questionnaire which they want us to answer by 10th July. The next step is to schedule a tailored demo focused on their tendering process, from supplier registration to sourcing event creation, supplier participation, evaluation, negotiation, and award. Alongside this, share a pricing questionnaire to help them estimate the potential cost. Internally, we should also check implementation support options for Greece, including local integrator or Big Four partner possibilities. They have also gotten back to us with a techincal questionnaire which they want us to answer by 10th July.
hard.ais_why: Their upper management wants to make the organization future ready and hence a key need for this project is for the vendor to have AI capabilities. AI is a very important theme for them because they are looking for faster, more efficient, and auditable procurement processes. Their current setup is manual, with Excel and email, so AI can help reduce manual intervention, improve reporting, and support better decision-making. They are specifically interested in AI capabilities that can support procurement digitalization, sourcing, supplier management, and future contract-related processes.

north_star_verdict.headline: Live, buyer-driven early-stage evaluation. Piraeus Port Authority proactively issued a Business and Technical questionnaire on 30 June 2026 (responses due 10 July) and is pending a tailored Zycus demo, with AIS at 100 (Strong) because upper management wants AI-ready, auditable procurement to replace a manual Excel-and-email process. The deal is genuinely engaged and moving, not cold. It rests on two procurement and IT evaluators (Pantelis Kazatzis, Achilleas Spanos) with no economic buyer, executive sponsor, or champion mapped, and the procurement vehicle (sole-source vs a formal EU public tender) is unconfirmed. Close date 2027-03-31 is a comfortable 268 days out with ample runway for discovery, demo, and power mapping.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 16 June 2026; Qualified 29 June 2026; today 6 July 2026, so 7 days in Qualified. Close date 2027-03-31 is 268 days out. Close date was pushed OUT 2026-12-31 to 2027-03-31 on 29 June (a forward re-plan, not a slip against pace). Buyer-driven movement 30 June (questionnaire issued, demo requested). Required pace to Q1 2027 close: answer questionnaire (10 July), run tailored demo, map economic buyer, secure a champion, confirm procurement vehicle, reach Shortlisted within ~60-90 days. Current pace is consistent with an engaged early-stage deal; no slip required.
evidence: Buyer-initiated engagement 30 June 2026: Piraeus Port Authority shared a Business and Technical questionnaire and asked Zycus to respond by 10 July (SF Next_Step__c)
Buyer explicitly requested a tailored Zycus demo: 'Following our last conversation, we are also pending a demo of Zycus application' (SF Next_Step__c, 30 June 2026)
Stage advanced Initial Interest to Qualified on 29 June 2026 (SF OpportunityFieldHistory)
Close date re-set forward 2026-12-31 to 2027-03-31 on 29 June 2026 (SF OpportunityFieldHistory)
AIS 100 / Strong: upper management wants an AI-capable vendor for faster, auditable, digitalized procurement replacing a manual Excel-and-email process (SF AIS_Score__c / AIS_Status__c / AIS_Why__c)

Two OpportunityContactRole evaluators: Pantelis Kazatzis (Procurement Manager) and Achilleas Spanos (IT Business Analyst & Developer, primary); no Economic Buyer, Executive Sponsor, Decision Maker, or Champion role on record
Products in scope: iContract, iSource, iSupplier, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake (SF Products__c); no line-item pricing yet
No competitor named in any source (SF Competitors__c blank; zero calls; Next_Step has no rival)
Zero Avoma calls matched to this opportunity or account; engagement to date is document-and-email based
Greek public sector at 150k ACV: a formal EU competitive tender may be required, but the procurement vehicle is unconfirmed (no SF field, no call)
Internal note to check Greece implementation support (local integrator or Big Four partner) (SF Next_Step__c, 30 June 2026)

metrics.narrative: The buyer's business problem is clearly articulated: their procurement is run manually on Excel and email, and upper management wants a future-ready, AI-capable platform for faster, more efficient, and auditable procurement (SF AIS_Why__c). No numbers are yet attached, no cost of the manual process, no cycle-time or headcount figure, no savings target, so the value case is qualitative, not quantified. The 30 June Business Questionnaire asks Zycus to map functional, non-functional, and AI requirements across the lifecycle but is a capability checklist, not a quantified ROI case. The move is to run a value discovery with Pantelis Kazatzis on the pending demo and convert the manual-process pain into a euro cost and cycle-time figure that a budget owner will fund.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named on any Salesforce field, contact role, or call. The strongest clue is AIS_Why__c: 'upper management wants to make the organization future ready,' which places the sponsor at senior/executive level but leaves them unnamed. The two mapped contacts, Pantelis Kazatzis (Procurement Manager) and Achilleas Spanos (IT Business Analyst & Developer), are both Evaluators, not budget owners. The 30 June questionnaire itself asks Zycus to fill a 'License Cost (budget)' column per role, which signals a budget-scoping exercise is underway but does not reveal who signs. The move is to use the tailored demo to ask Pantelis who owns the budget and sign-off, and to secure that sponsor into the process.
sources: OpportunityContactRole (Pantelis Kazatzis, Achilleas Spanos, both Evaluators)
SF Next_Step__c 30 June 2026 (User Matrix / License Cost)

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer has documented and issued their evaluation criteria directly: the 30 June Business Questionnaire lists functional and business requirements covering both digitalization and AI functionalities across the entire procurement lifecycle, plus non-functional requirements, a user/role matrix with license type and cost, and specific reporting outputs (Reporting 1, Reporting 2, Procurement Plan). A separate Technical Questionnaire probes the AI architecture and technical capabilities of the software. This is an unusually explicit, buyer-supplied criteria set for such an early stage, and AI capability across sourcing, supplier management, and contracts is the standout weighted theme (AIS Strong). The criteria are confirmed; what is not yet known is the relative weighting and scoring rubric the buyer will apply.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 30 June 2026 (Business Questionnaire Reqs, User Matrix, Reporting sheets; Technical Questionnaire)

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a structured, document-led evaluation: they issued a formal Business and Technical questionnaire on 30 June with a hard 10 July response deadline, to be followed by a tailored demo of the tendering process from supplier registration through award. This is a deliberate, criteria-first process rather than an ad hoc look. What is not yet visible is the approval chain and the procurement vehicle: as a Greek public-sector port authority at 150k, a formal EU competitive tender may be mandated, which would open the field and lengthen the cycle. The move is to confirm on the demo whether this is a sole-source evaluation or a precursor to an open tender, and to map the approval and award timeline.
sources: Account: Piraeus Port Authority (public sector, Greece)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics (MSA, Order Form, SOW, legal review, InfoSec, procurement channel) are entirely unknown; no call or field describes them. The one concrete signal is an internal Zycus note in Next_Step__c (30 June) to check implementation support options for Greece, including a local integrator or Big Four partner, which flags that delivery in Greece will likely need a local partner but is a Zycus action item, not a stated buyer requirement. There is no Current_Contract_Expiration__c forcing function on record. As a public entity, any purchase will likely route through formal public-procurement rules with defined tender and award steps. The move is to establish the buyer's procurement and contracting path (tender vs direct, e-signature, delivery partner) once the demo confirms intent.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 30 June 2026 (Greece implementation support note)
Account: Piraeus Port Authority (public sector, Greece)

identify_pain.narrative: The core pain is confirmed qualitatively: procurement today is manual, run on Excel and email, with heavy manual intervention, weak reporting, and no AI support, and upper management wants faster, more efficient, auditable, AI-enabled procurement (SF AIS_Why__c). The pain owner is the procurement organization, with Pantelis Kazatzis (Procurement Manager) as the operational owner and unnamed upper management as the sponsor of change. What is inferred rather than confirmed is the operational and financial magnitude, no quote quantifies the cost of the manual process or a compelling event that forces action this fiscal year. The move is a value discovery to convert this articulated pain into a quantified, sponsor-ready case.
sources: OpportunityContactRole (Pantelis Kazatzis, Procurement Manager; Achilleas Spanos, IT Business Analyst & Developer)

champion.narrative: No champion exists yet. The two mapped contacts are Pantelis Kazatzis (Procurement Manager, Evaluator) and Achilleas Spanos (IT Business Analyst & Developer, Evaluator, primary). The buyer side has engaged constructively, issuing the questionnaire and requesting a demo, but that is a formal evaluation posture, not personal advocacy: there is no evidence of anyone introducing Zycus to power, sharing internal context, or pushing Zycus's case. Pantelis is the natural champion candidate as the procurement process owner, and Achilleas is the technical validator for the AI-architecture criteria. The move is to convert Pantelis into a champion through a value discovery and by helping him build the internal business case, then earn an introduction to the budget owner.

competition.narrative: No competitor is named in any source: Competitors__c is blank, the Next_Step carries no rival, and there are zero calls where the buyer mentioned other vendors. The 30 June questionnaire is vendor-neutral and structured like a requirements-gathering document, which is consistent with either a sole-source scoping exercise or preparation for a broader tender. As a Greek public-sector port authority at 150k ACV, a formal EU competitive tender is plausible and would introduce rivals through an open RFP, but none is confirmed, so the competitive picture is genuinely invisible rather than clear. The move is to establish on the demo whether Zycus is being evaluated alone or against others, and if others, who and why, before positioning the Merlin AI and agentic-procurement wedge.
sources: Account: Piraeus Port Authority (public sector, Greece, 150k)

deal_movement.summary: The deal is early but genuinely moving on buyer initiative. In the last week it advanced to Qualified (29 June), had its close date re-planned forward to 2027-03-31, and the buyer issued a formal Business and Technical questionnaire on 30 June with a 10 July response deadline plus a pending demo request. Engagement is document-and-email based; no live call has yet occurred.

items.change: Stage advanced Initial Interest to Qualified
Buyer shared Business and Technical questionnaire; Zycus responses due 10 July; tailored demo pending
Opportunity created and owner set to Caroline Lacocque

competitive_position.summary: No competitor is named in any source as of 6 July 2026: Competitors__c is blank, there are zero calls, and the Next_Step names no rival. The competitive picture is unknown, not clear. The 30 June Business and Technical questionnaire is vendor-neutral and could precede either a sole-source award or a formal EU public tender; at 150k in Greek public sector a formal tender is plausible and would open the field to procurement suites with local references. The single most important competitive action is not to position against a named rival but to confirm the procurement vehicle and, if it is competitive, learn who is in it before the tailored demo. No rival is listed because none appears in real evidence; inventing one would be fabrication.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on the two things the buyer weights most, AI-enabled procurement and digitalizing a manual process, with an open question on local Greek delivery support.

best_practice_check.summary: Win-strategy levers for a live but power-thin early-stage AI evaluation.
flags: Win the questionnaire and pull the tailored tendering demo forward: answer the AI and functional requirements precisely to turn a neutral evaluation into a Zycus lean before 10 July.
Map power now: no economic buyer or upper-management sponsor is named; use the demo to reach the budget owner behind 'upper management wants to be future ready'.
Confirm the procurement vehicle early: at 150k in Greek public sector an EU formal tender may be mandated, so learn the rules before over-investing in a single-threaded path.

items.risk: Process owner and natural champion candidate but not yet advocating; deal is single-threaded on procurement if he does not convert.

items.name: Achilleas Spanos
items.title: IT Business Analyst & Developer
items.risk: Primary contact and the AI-architecture / technical-questionnaire evaluator; if IT is not satisfied on AI architecture, technical validation stalls.

champion_strength.summary: No champion yet. Pantelis Kazatzis (Procurement Manager) is the strongest candidate as the process owner who issued the questionnaire, and Achilleas Spanos (IT Business Analyst & Developer) is the technical evaluator, but neither has shown advocacy, introduced Zycus to power, or pushed Zycus's case. The buyer's engagement is procedural, not personal sponsorship. Building Pantelis into a champion through a value discovery and helping him author the internal business case is the most urgent early-stage action, ahead of any commercial step.
champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole (Pantelis Kazatzis, Achilleas Spanos); SF Next_Step__c 30 June 2026; zero Avoma calls

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is the central, buyer-weighted theme (AIS 100, Strong) and Merlin plus Merlin Intake are already in scope, so Zycus is well positioned on paper. The AI story is not yet landed live, though, because no demo has run; the tailored demo must make the Merlin AI and agentic-procurement narrative concrete against the Technical Questionnaire's AI-architecture questions.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 100 / Strong: upper management wants an AI-capable vendor for faster, auditable, digitalized procurement to replace a manual Excel-and-email process, and the buyer's own Technical Questionnaire probes AI architecture. Strong, buyer-confirmed AI appetite; no calls yet to over- or under-rate it against.

Greece delivery may require a local integrator or Big Four partner that is not yet lined up.

confidence_signals.summary: Buyer just engaged on 30 June with a formal questionnaire and demo request; this is a warming, not cooling, early-stage deal, though depth is unproven without a live call.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer proactively issued a detailed questionnaire and requested a tailored Zycus demo with a hard 10 July deadline (positive pull), but stated no lean toward Zycus over any rival (SF Next_Step__c, 30 June 2026).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain is clear (manual Excel-and-email procurement; upper management wants AI-enabled, auditable, faster processes) but no cost, cycle-time, or savings figure is quantified (SF AIS_Why__c).

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Piraeus Port Authority is running a formal, AI-first procurement evaluation and just sent Zycus a business and technical questionnaire due 10 July plus a demo request, so the deal is genuinely live; the only things between us and progress are the missing economic buyer and champion and an unconfirmed procurement vehicle.

win_position.text: Live, buyer-driven evaluation: Piraeus Port Authority proactively issued a detailed AI and functional questionnaire on 30 June and is pending a tailored Zycus demo, with AIS at 100 (Strong) because upper management wants AI-ready, auditable procurement to replace Excel and email.

No economic buyer, executive sponsor, or champion is named; the deal rests on two evaluators, Pantelis Kazatzis (Procurement Manager) and Achilleas Spanos (IT Business Analyst), with no one advocating internally.
The procurement vehicle is unconfirmed; at 150k in Greek public sector a formal EU tender could open the field to rivals Zycus has not yet seen (Next Step, 30 June).

deal_momentum.text: Buyer set concrete dates and pulled the process: questionnaire responses due 10 July and a tailored tendering demo pending, both buyer-initiated not seller-pushed (Next Step, 30 June).
Commercials are entering early, the buyer's own questionnaire asks Zycus to fill license type and cost per role (Next Step, 30 June).
Engagement is entirely document-and-email so far, no live discovery or demo call has happened yet, so depth of intent is unproven.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer invested real effort building and issuing a structured Business and Technical questionnaire across the full procurement lifecycle, signalling a funded project not a browse (30 June).
Commitment is procedural, not preferential: the buyer stated no lean toward Zycus and no budget owner is confirmed.

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded to two evaluators, Pantelis Kazatzis and Achilleas Spanos, with no champion and no economic-buyer access as of 6 July.
Greece delivery may require a local integrator or Big Four partner that is not yet lined up (Next Step, 30 June).
Competitive landscape is invisible; if this becomes an EU tender, procurement suites with local references could enter.

champion_coverage.evidence: No champion; only two neutral evaluators, no advocacy or access to power (OpportunityContactRole; Next Step 30 June).

economic_buyer_access.evidence: Economic buyer unnamed; change driven by unnamed upper management (AIS_Why__c).

engagement.evidence: Buyer-issued questionnaire with a 10 July deadline and a pending demo request (Next Step, 30 June).

contributions.evidence: customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).
Next Step carries 2 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 20 is carried by momentum 68 vs stage-expected 50; preference strength +0.40 (weight 20). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 68 (moving forward): commercial topics entering (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 14 is anchored on the stage win (20), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 68, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
